description_long: This object is a delicate Phalaenopsis orchid spray, featuring several large, showy flowers and unopened buds along a slender, dark green stem. The flowers display broad, overlapping petals with a soft lavender-pink hue, accented by darker purple veining and a striking magenta and yellow central column (labellum). The petals are rounded and slightly ruffled, arranged in a bilateral symmetry typical of orchids, with the central labellum protruding forward. The unopened buds are oval and tinged with green and yellow, gradually transitioning to the open blossoms. No leaves are visible on the stem, emphasizing the floral display. The overall appearance is elegant and ornamental, suitable for decorative use in urban parks, gardens, or indoor settings.
description: A spray of Phalaenopsis orchid flowers with broad, pinkish petals, purple veining, and several unopened buds on a slender stem. The flowers are large, showy, and arranged in a graceful, ornamental cluster.
description_view_0: Three open orchid flowers with prominent pink petals and purple veining are visible, along with several buds on a dark stem.
description_view_1: The view highlights the unopened buds and the side profile of the central flower, showing the layered petal arrangement.
description_view_2: The back of the flowers is visible, displaying the pale pink and white coloration of the petals and the alignment of the buds along the stem.
description_view_3: A side view showing the overlapping petals and the arrangement of buds, with the flowers facing away from the viewer.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a bilateral symmetry, with three broad outer sepals and two lateral petals overlapping slightly, and a prominent central labellum forming a distinct lower lip. The arrangement is typical of orchids, with layering and some overlapping of petals and sepals.
